sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew, is a 30-year-old male who has been experiencing back pain for an extended period, with the pain worsening over the past month.
He reports that the pain is located in the lower back, radiating down the left leg, and describes it as a "electrical toothache" that worsens with standing, walking, coughing, and sneezing.
He rates his pain as 6 out of 10 currently, but it can be as high as 13 out of 10.
He mentions that laying down is the only position that brings relief.
Past medical history: The patient mentions a past injury from a fall off a roof 30 years ago, which may have resulted in a possible fracture.
However, no further information is provided about this incident.
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness and tingling in both lower extremities, with weakness in both legs.
He also mentions that coughing and sneezing exacerbate his symptoms.
Current medications: NOT MENTIONED
Objective:
Vital signs: NOT MENTIONED
Physical exam: The patient has pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae, and pain with flexion and extension of the back.
Strength in the left leg is 4 out of 5, and 5 out of 5 on the right.
Reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae, associated with some nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; lower back pain; leg pain; weakness in both legs.
Plan:
I recommended an epidural steroid injection to place anti-inflammatory medication at the site of inflammation.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
I want the patient to see physical therapy and return in about a month to reassess the effectiveness of the injection.
Follow-up: I want the patient to see me again in about a month to reassess the effectiveness of the injection.
